faq.question: Can foreigners open a company in Indonesia?
faq.answer: Yes. Foreigners can establish a PT PMA (foreign-owned company) in Indonesia.
faq.keywords: foreigners, non-Indonesians, PT PMA, start business

faq.question: What is a PT PMA?
faq.answer: A PT PMA is a foreign-owned company legally registered in Indonesia.

faq.question: How many shareholders are required?
faq.answer: Minimum two shareholders.

faq.question: What is the cost of company setup?
How long does company setup take?
faq.answer: About 5 working days.

faq.question: How many shareholders are required for a PMA company?
faq.answer: A minimum of two shareholders is required. Both shareholders can be foreigners.

faq.question: Can foreigners be directors of the company?
faq.answer: Yes. Foreign shareholders can act as Directors or Commissioners and sign company and bank documents.

faq.question: How long does it take to establish a PMA company?
faq.answer: The company setup usually takes 5 working days.

faq.question: What documents are needed to start a PMA company?
faq.answer: •	 Passport copies of two shareholders •	 Proposed company name (3 words) •	 Description of business activities

faq.question: What documents will the client receive after company setup?
faq.answer: •	 Company Deed (Akta Pendirian) •	 SK Kemenkumham (Legalization) •	 NIB (Business Identiﬁcation Number) •	 NPWP (Company Tax Number) •	 Business licenses (KBLI) •	 Company stamp •	 Soft copy and original hard copy documents

faq.question: Can company shareholders obtain a visa in Indonesia?
faq.answer: Yes. Shareholders can apply for a 2-year Investor Visa (KITAS Investor).

faq.question: What is the price of an investor visa?
faq.answer: IDR 18,500,000 per person.

faq.question: How long does the investor visa process take?
faq.answer: Around 5–7 working days after the company is established.

faq.question: What documents are required for the investor visa?
faq.answer: •	 Passport copy •	 Passport photo •	 Bank statement (minimum USD 2,000) •	 CV (Curriculum Vitae)

faq.question: Can the investor visa lead to permanent residency?
faq.answer: Yes. After several years, it may lead to a 5-year KITAP (permanent stay permit).

faq.question: How much does a PMA company setup cost?
faq.answer: IDR 30,000,000 (one-time fee).

faq.question: What does the company setup fee include?
faq.answer: •	 Notary services •	 Government registration •	 NIB & licenses •	 NPWP tax registration •	 Bank account assistance •	 Company documents

faq.question: Are there any additional beneﬁts included?
faq.answer: Yes. •	 3 months free virtual oﬃce •	 1 year free legal consultation •	 Support via WhatsApp and email

faq.question: Do companies need ongoing compliance services?
faq.answer: Yes. PMA companies must report taxes and submit regular reports.

faq.question: What services does Bali Legal Partner provide for maintenance?
faq.answer: •	 Monthly tax reporting •	 Accounting & bookkeeping •	 Payroll management •	 Annual tax reporting •	 LKPM investment reporting (quarterly)

faq.question: How much does ongoing compliance cost?
faq.answer: Starting from IDR 2,000,000 per month, depending on: •	 Number of invoices •	 Number of employees •	 Business activity volume

faq.question: What is KBLI?
faq.answer: KBLI is the Indonesian business classiﬁcation code used to deﬁne the business activities of a company.

faq.question: Who decides the KBLI code?
faq.answer: The client and Bali Legal Partner team decide together based on the business activity.

faq.question: Can foreigners operate villa rental businesses in Bali?
faq.answer: Yes, through a PMA company with proper licenses.

faq.question: What permits are required for villa rental?
faq.answer: Typical permits include: •	 IMB / PBG (Building Permit) •	 TDUP (Tourism Business License) •	 NIB (Business ID) •	 NPWPD (Local tax registration)

faq.question: Are there business sectors restricted for 100% foreign ownership?
faq.answer: Yes. Some sectors are restricted or closed. Examples include: •	 Wild capture ﬁshing •	 Small retail shops (warungs, kiosks) •	 Small-scale construction services •	 Local transportation services •	 Traditional media (TV, radio, newspapers)

faq.question: Can PMA companies do aquaculture?
faq.answer: Yes. Fish farming and aquaculture are allowed for PMA companies.

faq.question: Can PMA companies do wild ocean ﬁshing?
faq.answer: No. Wild capture ﬁshing in Indonesian waters is restricted to Indonesian companies.

faq.question: Can a PMA company rent property through Airbnb?
faq.answer: Yes, if the company has the correct KBLI codes and tourism licenses.

faq.question: Do companies need BPJS for employees?
faq.answer: Yes. Companies must register employees in: •	 BPJS Kesehatan (Health insurance) •	 BPJS Ketenagakerjaan (Employment insurance)

faq.question: What is KITAS?
faq.answer: KITAS is a 2-year investor visa that allows foreigners to live and work in Indonesia as a company shareholder. Cost is IDR 18,500,000.

faq.question: What is KITAP?
faq.answer: KITAP is a permanent stay permit. After several years on a KITAS investor visa, you may qualify for a 5-year KITAP.

faq.question: Can both shareholders be foreigners?
faq.answer: Yes. Both shareholders can be foreigners.

faq.question: Is 100% foreign ownership allowed?
faq.answer: Yes, depending on the business sector. Many sectors allow 100% foreign ownership.

faq.question: Do I need an Indonesian partner?
faq.answer: No. Many sectors allow full foreign ownership without an Indonesian partner.

faq.question: Do I need to be in Indonesia to open a company?
faq.answer: No. The entire process can be done remotely.

faq.question: Will I receive original documents?
faq.answer: Yes. You will receive both soft copy and original hard copy documents.

faq.question: What is NIB?
faq.answer: NIB is the Business Identification Number issued by the Indonesian government.

faq.question: What is NPWP?
faq.answer: NPWP is the company tax number in Indonesia.

faq.question: Can the investor visa be extended?
faq.answer: Yes. The investor visa can be extended.

faq.question: What bank balance is required for investor visa?
faq.answer: A minimum bank statement of USD 2,000 is required for the investor visa.

faq.question: What businesses can foreigners do in Indonesia?
faq.answer: Foreigners can operate in many sectors including tourism, consulting, trading, technology, restaurants, villas, Airbnb, and travel agencies through a PT PMA company.

faq.question: Can foreigners open restaurants in Indonesia?
faq.answer: Yes. Foreigners can open restaurants in Indonesia through a PT PMA company.

faq.question: Can foreigners open villas in Indonesia?
faq.answer: Yes. Foreigners can open and operate villas through a PT PMA company with proper licenses.

faq.question: Can foreigners do consulting businesses?
faq.answer: Yes. Consulting is open for foreign-owned PMA companies in Indonesia.

faq.question: Can foreigners open travel agencies?
faq.answer: Yes. Travel agencies are open for foreign-owned PMA companies.

faq.question: Can foreigners own small retail shops?
faq.answer: No. Small retail shops such as warungs and kiosks are restricted for foreign ownership.

faq.question: Can foreigners run small construction companies?
faq.answer: No. Small-scale construction services are restricted for foreign ownership.

faq.question: Can foreigners operate traditional taxis?
faq.answer: No. Local transportation services are restricted for foreign ownership.

faq.question: Can foreigners own TV stations?
faq.answer: No. Traditional media including TV, radio, and newspapers are restricted for foreign ownership.

faq.question: What is IMB or PBG?
faq.answer: IMB/PBG is a Building Permit required for villa and accommodation businesses in Indonesia.

faq.question: What is NPWPD?
faq.answer: NPWPD is the local tax registration for hotel or restaurant taxes in Indonesia.

faq.question: Can foreigners open company bank accounts?
faq.answer: Yes. Foreigners can open company bank accounts in Indonesia. Bali Legal Partner assists with this.

faq.question: Do you assist with bank account opening?
faq.answer: Yes. Bank account assistance is included in the company setup package.

faq.question: Do companies need monthly tax reporting?
faq.answer: Yes. PMA companies must submit monthly tax reports in Indonesia.

faq.question: Do you provide accounting services?
faq.answer: Yes. We provide monthly tax reporting, accounting, bookkeeping, payroll management, and annual tax reporting.

faq.question: What is LKPM?
faq.answer: LKPM is the quarterly investment report required for all PMA companies in Indonesia.

faq.question: Do companies need annual tax reporting?
faq.answer: Yes. Annual tax reporting is required for all PMA companies.

faq.question: Do I need an office address for my company?
faq.answer: Yes. A registered office address is required. Bali Legal Partner provides 3 months free virtual office with company setup.

faq.question: Can foreigners buy property in Indonesia?
faq.answer: Foreigners can invest in property through specific ownership structures via a PT PMA company.

faq.question: Can PMA companies lease land?
faq.answer: Yes. PMA companies can lease land in Indonesia.

faq.question: Can PMA companies develop villas?
faq.answer: Yes. PMA companies can develop villas with proper permits.

faq.question: Do you assist with villa permits?
faq.answer: Yes. We assist with all villa permits including TDUP, IMB/PBG, NIB, and NPWPD.

faq.question: Do you assist with alcohol licenses?
faq.answer: Yes. Bali Legal Partner assists with alcohol licenses.

faq.question: How long does company setup take?
faq.answer: Company setup takes 5 working days.

faq.question: How long does visa processing take?
faq.answer: Investor visa processing takes 5 to 7 working days after company is established.

faq.question: How long do business permits take?
faq.answer: Business permits typically take 2 to 6 weeks depending on approvals.

faq.question: How are you?
faq.answer: Hello! I am the Bali Legal Partner assistant. I help foreigners with PT PMA company setup, investor visas, and business services in Indonesia. How can I help you today?
faq.keywords: how are you, who are you, hello, hi, greetings

faq.question: Where do I send my documents?
faq.answer: Please send your passport copies and company name directly to our team via WhatsApp or email. Our team will guide you through the next steps.

faq.question: Are there hidden fees?
faq.answer: No hidden fees. IDR 30,000,000 is the complete all-inclusive fee. It includes notary services, government registration, NIB, NPWP, bank account assistance, and all company documents.

faq.question: How do I know Bali Legal Partner is trustworthy?
faq.answer: Bali Legal Partner has assisted many international clients with company setup, investor visas, and business licensing in Indonesia. We provide 1 year free legal consultation and ensure full compliance with Indonesian law.

faq.question: How much for company and visa together?
faq.answer: The combined cost is IDR 48,500,000. This includes IDR 30,000,000 for company setup and IDR 18,500,000 for the investor visa.
faq.keywords: company, visa, together, combined, total, both
